label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004 would be the next one. So 2001's February had 28 days. So Jane was born on 02/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So we need to figure out what today's date is. If she was born on 02/28/2001, then her 16th birthday would be 16 years later, so 02/28/2017. Let me check: 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today is 02/28/2017. Now, the question asks, what is the date 10 days ago from today? So if today is 02/28/2017, subtracting 10 days from that date. Let me count backwards. Starting from Feb 28, 2017. Let's see, February 2017: how many days does it have? 2017 is not a leap year (since 2017 divided by 4 is 504.25, not an integer), so February has 28 days. So if today is the 28th, then 10 days ago would be... Let's subtract 10 days from 28. 28 - 10 = 18. So that would be February 18, 2017. So 02/18/2017. But wait, I should check if that's correct. Let me count each day to make sure. Starting from today (Feb 28) as day 0, then: - 1 day ago: Feb 27 - 2 days ago: Feb 26 - 3: Feb 25 - 4: Feb 24 - 5: Feb 23 - 6: Feb 22 - 7: Feb 21 - 8: Feb 20 - 9: Feb 19 - 10 days ago: Feb 18 Yes, that's correct. So 10 days ago from Feb 28, 2017, was Feb 18, 2017. So the answer should be option B, which is 02/18/2017. Let me check the options again. The options are: (A) 02/28/2017 → that's today, not 10
